capabilities.name: Enclave signing
capabilities.exclusion: Software-backed and simulated drivers are test/development-only and cannot satisfy the typed production policy; no real provider verifier or exact release artifact is available.
capabilities.notes: The reviewed checkpoint adds fail-closed typed value-bearing requests/responses, canonical operation and key binding, settlement purpose/domain/context checks, typed settlement propagation, raw-signing exclusion, and preserves PR #216 signer-identity evidence. Provider-issued hardware evidence, deployment integration, independent review, and artifact evidence remain open.

capabilities.name: Enclave hardware attestation
capabilities.exclusion: Simulated fixtures and software attestation tests do not establish vendor-root, deployed-hardware, provider/runtime, or release-artifact support.
capabilities.notes: Reviewed fix commit 99f8415be19d59ed4a2e5a07ff66b70902ede106 covers the Phase A provider-neutral bounded trust transport, exact production policy/verifier binding, centralized signer-anchor checks, explicit SingleMechanismAttestationResult scope, trusted-clock context replacement before provider observation/canonicalization, and privacy-safe audit metadata. The normalized result is one-mechanism evidence only; it does not satisfy the six-factor policy. The provider extension seam is crate-private/test-only and external adapters are not enabled. This is local code/test contract evidence only; provider verifiers, vendor roots/collateral, deployed hardware/runtime, independent review, and release artifacts remain unavailable, so production support is unchanged.

capabilities.name: Typed proof-factor taxonomy and composition
capabilities.exclusion: Server identity, user authorization, phone/device attestation, TEE attestation, FIDO2/WebAuthn assertions, and TPM quotes are taxonomy and fail-closed composition support only. The production registry has no concrete provider verifier; test fixtures are test-only and cannot satisfy a production policy. No production server, user, phone, TEE, FIDO2, TPM, StrongBox, collateral, runtime, distributed replay, independent-review, or release-artifact support is claimed.
capabilities.notes: Phase A adds bounded raw evidence, private-field exact verified proof types, canonical domain-separated contexts, independent typed verifier dispatch, and explicit all-required proof-set composition. SingleMechanismAttestationResult and SingleMechanismReplayAuthorization are explicitly scoped to one mechanism and cannot satisfy or convert into complete all-required authorization; only the explicit proof-bundle composition path can do so. Existing DeviceIntegrityReport remains the current device/TEE adapter and is never silently upgraded into the new proof categories.

capabilities.name: Attestation replay and freshness protection
capabilities.exclusion: Replay authorization is process-local and fixture-tested; it is not distributed replay coordination or exact-artifact production evidence.
capabilities.notes: Reviewed fix commit 99f8415be19d59ed4a2e5a07ff66b70902ede106 covers the separate SingleMechanismReplayIdentity and SingleMechanismReplayAuthorization types, explicit single-mechanism scope binding, bounded idempotency key, atomic-store outcome contract, trusted-clock wrapper, expiry-before-store checks, and process-local rollback high-water checks. Durable replay accepts only a single-mechanism normalized result and cannot return complete all-required/value-bearing authorization. Existing ProofReplayKey and ReplayGuard remain unchanged; no durable backend, distributed replay coordination, provider availability, independent review, or release-artifact support is claimed.

capabilities.name: Rail policy and attestation enforcement
capabilities.exclusion: Typed fixture authorization, process-local replay, and local rail tests are containment evidence only; no real provider-backed rail settlement support is claimed.
capabilities.notes: Raw production settlement dispatch is rejected and built-in rails receive typed verified-operation envelopes carrying canonical intent, operation/key binding, signature, attestation, provenance, policy, and replay authorization. Real provider verification, distributed replay, independent review, and artifacts remain open.

capabilities.name: Settlement rail adapters
capabilities.exclusion: Built-in adapter dispatch is explicitly disabled before request-body serialization and network I/O. Adapter interfaces and local tests do not establish live-network, address-provenance, or settlement support.
capabilities.notes: Rail families are inventoried together because their shared policy boundary is the production gate. Typed settlement authorization containment is present and requires the Settlement purpose, canonical settlement domain, and canonical intent digest context; built-in adapter dispatch remains disabled pending a versioned wire contract and gateway compatibility evidence. The legacy raw-signature shim remains rejected.

capabilities.name: Bitcoin ECDSA signing
capabilities.exclusion: Software signer and unit-vector evidence is not hardware-backed production support.
capabilities.notes: Canonical verification and hardware-backed signing remain separate gates.

capabilities.name: Bitcoin Schnorr and Taproot
capabilities.exclusion: This row covers canonical BIP-340 verification and BIP-341/BIP-86 x-only output-key derivation only; Taproot script-path/Tapscript execution, hardware/provider signing, independent review, release artifacts, and production support are not evidenced.
capabilities.notes: The reviewed code uses canonical BIP-340 verification and BIP-341/BIP-86 tagged output-key derivation, including full-key to x-only normalization and odd-Y secret normalization before TapTweak in the software signing path. Official BIP-340, BIP-341, and BIP-86 vectors pass; these vectors and local tests do not establish script-path execution, hardware evidence, release evidence, or production support.

capabilities.name: BIP-322 message verification
capabilities.exclusion: Verification is limited to native P2WPKH and native P2TR key-path Simple signatures; no Bitcoin Script/Tapscript interpreter, Full or Proof-of-Funds verification, hardware/provider runtime evidence, independent review, release artifact, or production support is established.
capabilities.notes: Native P2WPKH and native P2TR key-path Simple signatures have cryptographic verification with typed network-aware outcomes and official BIP-322 basic/generated vectors. P2WSH and Taproot script-path/annex paths return explicit inconclusive outcomes, while Full and Proof-of-Funds payloads return dedicated typed unsupported errors. Construction helpers and BIP-110 checks do not expand this verification boundary.

capabilities.name: BIP-110 reduced-data compliance
capabilities.exclusion: Local compliance and chunking tests do not prove complete canonical Bitcoin signing or release support.
capabilities.notes: The feature is recorded as an API/implementation aid, not as a production release decision.

capabilities.name: Ethereum address and signed-message handling
capabilities.exclusion: This row does not cover full Ethereum transaction serialization, EIP-1559, EIP-712, provider/hardware/runtime evidence, independent review, release artifacts, or production support.
capabilities.notes: The row is scoped to address and signed-message behavior: canonical Keccak address derivation, strict EIP-55 mixed-case validation, EIP-191 text/binary hashing, low-S and recovery-parity validation, explicit EIP-2098 decoding, and context-bound EIP-155 `v` decoding are covered by official and negative vectors. Transaction construction and production authorization remain outside the evidenced scope.

capabilities.name: Solana address and transfer preparation
capabilities.exclusion: Local address/transfer preparation is not live-network or asset-provenance evidence.
capabilities.notes: Solana is inventoried independently from generic chain abstraction.

capabilities.name: Stacks address and transaction handling
capabilities.exclusion: API and local tests do not prove canonical address provenance or executable asset support.
capabilities.notes: Stacks remains conditional until chain-specific vectors and metadata evidence are attached.

capabilities.name: Multi-chain intent and chain abstraction
capabilities.exclusion: Cross-chain API tests are not proof of canonical per-chain authorization, routing, or settlement.
capabilities.notes: Chain abstraction is kept distinct from each chain's cryptographic and metadata evidence.

capabilities.name: Asset registry and chain metadata
capabilities.exclusion: Catalog/API completeness and registry tests do not validate executable address provenance or settlement safety.
capabilities.notes: Display/catalog metadata must remain distinct from active, verified executable assets.

capabilities.name: FROST threshold signing
capabilities.exclusion: Typed envelopes, validation, and quarantine tests are not RFC-compatible FROST cryptography or production signing evidence.
capabilities.notes: PR #209 establishes a versioned structural boundary only; RFC 9591, ciphersuite, DKG, nonce, zeroization, provider, attestation, and vector gates remain open.

capabilities.name: MuSig2 multi-signature aggregation
capabilities.exclusion: Wrapper/API evidence and local aggregation tests do not establish independent protocol review or production key custody.
capabilities.notes: MuSig2 is tracked separately from FROST because the protocol and evidence requirements differ.

capabilities.name: Fedimint and Nexus federation adapter
capabilities.exclusion: Provider-owned handles, federation schemas, operation ledgers, and quarantine tests are not production Fedimint cryptographic, database, or federation evidence.
capabilities.notes: PR #209 removes the public serialized note secret and establishes typed/provider/persistence boundaries. DLEQ is not asserted as inherent to every current canonical issuance path; implementation, share verification, backup, and artifact gates remain open.

capabilities.name: Ark vTXO and recovery orchestration
capabilities.exclusion: Typed IDs, descriptors, and structural validation are not live Ark interoperability, settlement, recovery, or unilateral-exit evidence.
capabilities.notes: PR #209 quarantines derivation, tree construction, forfeit signing, and recovery. `ArkManager::try_with_backend` and compatibility `with_backend` reject `ProviderOwned` with exact `ProtocolUnsupported`; `new` remains `Unconfigured`. A future milestone must choose and pin Arkade or Second after issue #195 provider/attestation evidence; inspected Arkade v0.9.15 remains Alpha/do-not-use-in-production.

capabilities.name: BitVM challenge primitives
capabilities.exclusion: Challenge/commitment APIs and structural tests do not prove SNARK verification, Bitcoin interoperability, or production settlement.
capabilities.notes: BitVM and BitVM2 remain separate records while sharing the threshold/settlement blocker. The native legacy `BitVmManager` is a non-production MuSig2 primitive; its generic signatures and aggregates are not BitVM2 evidence, and the public WASM compatibility surface is quarantined separately.

capabilities.name: BitVM2 challenge orchestration
capabilities.exclusion: Typed roles, observations, windows, and WASM mappings do not prove bridge graph correctness, proof/disprove verification, Bitcoin interoperability, or provider/runtime support.
capabilities.notes: PR #209 permits only externally observed, idempotent modeled events; posting, challenge, resolution, signing, and transaction construction remain unsupported. Legacy WASM BitVM signing and aggregation now fail before decoding with exact BitVM2 `ProtocolUnsupported` errors mapped to `PROTOCOL_UNSUPPORTED`; no generic MuSig2 output is treated as BitVM2 evidence.

capabilities.name: CCTP transfer and attestation
capabilities.exclusion: Empty payload and non-empty-attestation acceptance behavior is explicitly not production CCTP support.
capabilities.notes: CCTP remains disabled pending canonical message and attestation validation.

capabilities.name: Account abstraction
capabilities.exclusion: Structural batch preparation and non-empty module validation are not canonical account-abstraction execution evidence.
capabilities.notes: Unsupported modules must fail closed before production enablement.

capabilities.name: Bitcoin covenant and OP_CAT helpers
capabilities.exclusion: Structural script generation and local invariant tests do not prove consensus activation, vectors, or production covenant support.
capabilities.notes: Covenant APIs remain conditional on canonical Bitcoin evidence.

capabilities.name: Job Card and ISO 20022
capabilities.exclusion: XML/unit and WASM build evidence is not partner, settlement-network, runtime, or production artifact evidence.
capabilities.notes: The Job Card record covers the ISO 20022 wrapper and the native validation boundary.

capabilities.name: Lightning payment intent
capabilities.exclusion: Payment state-machine tests and WASM build surfaces are not LND/provider, runtime, hardware, or production settlement evidence.
capabilities.notes: Lightning remains conditional despite the presence of a public API and local lifecycle tests.

capabilities.name: Discreet Log Contracts
capabilities.exclusion: Contract lifecycle API tests and a WASM wrapper are not oracle, adaptor, Bitcoin, or production DLC evidence.
capabilities.notes: DLC is explicit in the matrix even though its runtime/provider lane is not present.

capabilities.name: MMR and state proofs
capabilities.exclusion: Local MMR/state tests and API wrappers do not establish deployed state-service, runtime, or artifact support.
capabilities.notes: MMR/state evidence is distinct from the Opportunity and Business records that consume it.

capabilities.name: Business registry and profiles
capabilities.exclusion: In-memory registry/API and WASM build evidence do not establish consumer integration, runtime, or production support.
capabilities.notes: Business is recorded as an API surface, not as a verified operational registry.

capabilities.name: Settlement service orchestration
capabilities.exclusion: Service orchestration and unit tests do not establish live settlement, hardware, partner, or exact-artifact evidence.
capabilities.notes: Settlement remains blocked by the shared protocol, attestation, runtime, and acceptance gates.

capabilities.name: Stablecoin orchestration
capabilities.exclusion: Stablecoin API and WASM compilation are not issuer, asset, runtime, or settlement evidence.
capabilities.notes: The explicit stablecoin record prevents an aggregate WASM row from implying provider support.

capabilities.name: Opportunity discovery
capabilities.exclusion: Dispatcher/API and WASM build evidence do not establish market, counterparty, or production decision support.
capabilities.notes: Opportunity is explicit because it is a named WASM sub-client and business-facing API. Its typed settlement preflight is validation-only in non-test builds and reaches the provider boundary; the default software/unverified provider still fails closed, so no production support is claimed.

capabilities.name: Solver bid ranking
capabilities.exclusion: Local ranking and WASM compilation do not establish solver network, economic, or production support evidence.
capabilities.notes: Solver is explicit and separate from swap routing and settlement orchestration.

capabilities.name: Swap router
capabilities.exclusion: Gateway URL/client construction and WASM build evidence do not establish live rail routing or production settlement.
capabilities.notes: Swap routing remains disabled from production claims until adapter and boundary evidence exists.

capabilities.name: Zero-knowledge machine learning
capabilities.exclusion: Service construction and WASM build evidence do not establish proof-system, model, runtime, or production evidence.
capabilities.notes: ZKML is inventoried even though current work is API-level and research-oriented.

capabilities.name: Identity profiles
capabilities.exclusion: Identity creation fails closed until a provider-verified identity-attestation API exists; software and development managers cannot produce an IdentityProfile or hardware-attestation claim.
capabilities.notes: The value-bearing containment boundary is implemented separately, but real provider-backed identity attestation remains unsupported.

capabilities.name: Credit and vouch intents
capabilities.exclusion: Deterministic intent preparation is not credit-provider, underwriting, compliance, or production evidence.
capabilities.notes: Credit remains a conditional API family with no independent review or provider integration record.

capabilities.name: Fiat session preparation
capabilities.exclusion: Fiat session API tests are not payment-provider, compliance, banking, or production artifact evidence.
capabilities.notes: No fiat provider integration or independent review is established in this repository.

capabilities.name: Economy and incentive helpers
capabilities.exclusion: Local incentive/helper tests do not establish economic model review, external integration, or production support.
capabilities.notes: Economy is recorded as a material source family but has no current production evidence chain.

capabilities.name: SIDL governance and voting
capabilities.exclusion: Serialization and local voting tests are not governance deployment, quorum, or production support evidence.
capabilities.notes: SIDL remains a conditional API family with no independent review or external governance artifact.

capabilities.name: Application-to-protocol (A2P)
capabilities.exclusion: OTP/intention API and an empty WASM client are not application integration, secret-boundary, or production evidence.
capabilities.notes: A2P is explicit because it is named by repository analysis and the WASM requirements.

capabilities.name: Telemetry and observability
capabilities.exclusion: Telemetry remains opt-in and best effort; independent review, server-side retention/access/deletion evidence, deployment monitoring, named on-call assignment, rollback-drill evidence, and production artifacts are not established by this documentation phase.
capabilities.notes: The code/test checkpoint is c976c377aaf3ac0d825e47fa1fd1436802fc130e and includes native credential-redirect hardening plus regression coverage that verifies redirects are not followed and the sensitive API key is not forwarded. This is SDK-local implementation/test evidence only; it does not establish production support or independent review. Payloads are minimized to the exact coarse event contract and credentials remain sensitive transport headers only. The new runbooks are SDK-local public-safe evidence and do not prove service-side controls. Telemetry must not become a hidden dependency for signing or other security decisions.

capabilities.name: CI, release, SBOM, and provenance evidence
capabilities.exclusion: The committed controls are implementation evidence only; a supported release still requires exact tagged-artifact, registry, retained CI, provenance, SBOM, and independent-review evidence.
capabilities.notes: Issue #145 is historical CI baseline context; #199 hardening is implemented here, while final release acceptance remains external evidence tracked by #202.

capabilities.notes: The explicit row records the binding without promoting it beyond the open WASM evidence gate.

capabilities.name: Attestation collateral and revocation verification
capabilities.exclusion: Phase A is provider-neutral trust-material validation and status binding only; it is not provider collateral, root, CRL/status, TCB, metadata, freshness, revocation-service, or production evidence.
capabilities.notes: Reviewed fix commit 99f8415be19d59ed4a2e5a07ff66b70902ede106 covers provider-neutral bounded collateral snapshot validation, payload/signature/digest/revision/window/status binding, signer-anchor authorization in the test-only positive route, single-mechanism normalized-result binding, trusted-clock freshness substitution, and unavailable production verifier routes. It does not evidence provider collateral/root/CRL/TCB/metadata freshness, revocation service, deployed integration, independent review, or release artifacts.
